Research & Achievement

From a Personal Challenge to 1st Place — My URC Research Journey

Meredith T. Villaluz Early 2026 5 min read

I made a commitment to myself at the start of this semester: to step outside my comfort zone and join a competition — the Undergraduate Research Conference at Brigham Young University–Hawaii. I didn't know exactly where it would lead, but I knew I had to try.

At first, it felt daunting. I was balancing three major classes that demanded significant time and focus. Still, I felt both nervous and excited — and I decided to take the leap.

The People Who Made It Possible

I'm deeply grateful to my mentor, Dr. Michael Morris, Ph.D., who helped me turn a broad interest into a focused research question. His guidance — from using AI tools to introducing me to Zotero and encouraging me to strengthen my methodology — played a huge role in shaping this work. I truly appreciate the knowledge and support he shared.

I also want to recognize my husband, who stepped up in the most meaningful way. He took care of our baby most of the time so I could focus on research. I couldn't have done this without his support.

"What started as a personal challenge of proving to myself that I could do more turned into something much bigger."

The Journey: 60–72 Hours of Grit

This journey took around 60–72 hours of reading articles, reviewing journals and policies, and writing drafts. In the middle of it all, the Kona Storm hit, the power went out, and the deadline was approaching fast. I honestly thought I wouldn't make it. But I kept going.

Research at a Glance
60–72
Hours of Research & Writing
🥇
1st Place — Business Research Award
1
Original Framework (FIRSM)
BSP
Policy Evaluated

The Result

I would also like to express my sincere thanks to the Undergraduate Research Conference committee at BYU–Hawaii for organizing this meaningful platform for students to share their work, learn from one another, and grow as researchers.

I'm honored to share that I received 1st Place in the Business Research Award at the conference. More than the result, this experience taught me something lasting: with grit, focus, and the willingness to try, we're capable of far more than we think.

"Excited to keep learning, growing, and pushing my limits."
URC 2026 Research 1st Place Financial Inclusion BYUH Philippines FIRSM
